Label: palladium
Definition: Chemical element (nickel group element atom) with atomic number 46.

Label: mole fraction
Definition: A concentration unit which denotes the number of moles of solute as a proportion of the total number of moles in a solution.

Label: containing magnetic species
Definition: A material property that inheres in a material entity in virtue of the presence of one or more magnetic species (e.g. ferromagnetic, paramagnetic, or diamagnetic particles or atoms), and that manifests under appropriate conditions as the ability of that material entity to exhibit a magnetic response or influence in a magnetic field.

Label: hardenable
Definition: a disposition to gain a greatly increased hardness at the surface or entire volume by the process of hardening

Label: atom
Definition: A chemical entity constituting the smallest component of an element having the chemical properties of the element.

Label: rhodium atom
Definition: A cobalt group element atom of atomic number 45.

Label: livermorium atom
Definition: A chalcogen atom with a symbol Lv and atomic number 116.

Label: mass percentage
Definition: A dimensionless concentration unit which denotes the mass of a substance in a mixture as a percentage of the mass of the entire mixture.

Label: polyurethane macromolecule
Definition: A homopolymer macromolecule composed of units connected by carbamate (-O-CO-NH-) linkages.

Label: iodine atom
Definition: Chemical element with atomic number 53.

Label: chromium atom
Definition: A chromium group element atom that has atomic number 24.

Label: polymerisation monomer
Definition: Any compound used as a monomer for a polymerisation process. The term is generally used in relation to industrial polymerisation processes.

Label: investigation agent role
Definition: A role borne by an entity and that is realized in a process that is part of an investigation in which an objective is achieved. These processes include, among others: planning, overseeing, funding, reviewing.

Label: roentgenium atom
Definition: A copper group element atom with atomic number 111. The the ninth member of the 6d series of transition metals, it is an extremely radioactive, synthetic element. Average mass is around 281.

Label: scalar value specification
Definition: A value specification that consists of two parts: a numeral and a unit label

Label: metal atom
Definition: An atom of an element that exhibits typical metallic properties, being typically shiny, with high electrical and thermal conductivity.

Label: cobalt atom
Definition: A cobalt group element atom that has atomic number 27.

Label: moscovium atom
Definition: A pnictogen atom with a symbol Mc and atomic number 115.

Label: copernicium atom
Definition: A zinc group element atom with a symbol Cn and atomic number 112. All its isotopes are intensely radioactive. Prior to its discovery, it had the placeholder name ununbium (in accordance with IUPAC recommendations). Following its discovery (in Darmstadt, 1996) and subsequent confirmation, the name copernicium was adopted in 2010.

Label: lead oxide
Definition: An inorganic lead salt composed from lead(2+) and oxide.

Label: thallium atom
Definition: A metallic element first identified and named from the brilliant green line in its flame spectrum (from Greek θαλλοσ, a green shoot).

Label: nihonium atom
Definition: A boron group element atom with a symbol Nh and atomic number 113.

Label: determination if assay will provide reliable results
Definition: A planned process that is used to assess whether an assay will provide reliable results based on the conditions or qualities of the inputs, devices, and other participants of the assay.

Label: silicon dioxide
Definition: A silicon oxide made up of linear triatomic molecules in which a silicon atom is covalently bonded to two oxygens.

Label: mass volume percentage
Definition: A dimensionless concentration unit which denotes the mass of the substance in a mixture as a percentage of the volume of the entire mixture.

Label: oganesson atom
Definition: A p-block element atom with a symbol Og and atomic number 118.

Label: calcium oxide
Definition: A member of the class of calcium oxides of calcium and oxygen in a 1:1 ratio.

Label: gallium atom
Definition: A metallic element predicted as eka-aluminium by Mendeleev in 1870 and discovered by Paul-Emile Lecoq de Boisbaudran in 1875. Named in honour of France (Latin Gallia) and perhaps also from the Latin gallus cock, a translation of Lecoq.

Label: indium atom
Definition: A metallic element first identified and named from the brilliant indigo (Latin indicum) blue line in its flame spectrum.

Label: flerovium atom
Definition: A carbon group element atom with a symbol Fl and atomic number 114.

Label: pure substance
Definition: A pure substance is a chemical substance composed of multiple molecules, which are all of the same kind.

Label: nickel atom
Definition: Chemical element (nickel group element atom) with atomic number 28.

Label: chalcogen
Definition: Any p-block element belonging to the group 16 family of the periodic table.

Label: corrosion resistant
Definition: a disposition to withstand corrosive attack to a certain extend

Label: time unit
Definition: A unit which is a standard measure of the dimension in which events occur in sequence.

Label: unit
Definition: A unit of measurement is a standardized quantity of a physical quality.

Label: iron atom
Definition: An iron group element atom that has atomic number 26.

Label: polonium atom
Definition: A radioactive metallic element discovered in 1898 by Marie Sklodowska Curie and named after her home country, Poland (Latin Polonia).

Label: bioactive
Definition: A disposition that inheres in a material entity and is realized in an organismal context as a specific interaction with one or more biological systems, producing a measurable biological effect (e.g., therapeutic, toxic, or signaling outcome).

Label: beryllium atom
Definition: Alkaline earth metal atom with atomic number 4.

Label: metalloid atom
Definition: An atom of an element that exhibits properties that are between those of metals and nonmetals, or that has a mixture of them. The term generally includes boron, silicon, germanium, arsenic, antimony, and tellurium, while carbon, aluminium, selenium, polonium, and astatine are less commonly included.

Label: macromolecule
Definition: A macromolecule is a molecule of high relative molecular mass, the structure of which essentially comprises the multiple repetition of units derived, actually or conceptually, from molecules of low relative molecular mass.

Label: diboron trioxide
Definition: A boron oxide with formula B<small><sub>2</sub></small>O<small><sub>3</sub></small>.

Label: tennessine atom
Definition: A halogen atom with a symbol Ts and atomic number 117.

Label: dielectric disposition
Definition: The disposition of an electric insulator to be polarized when subjected to an electric field.
